zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Vernon於大約1317年出生在英國英格蘭藍卡爾洛斯托可時，他的父親Richard de Vernon II33歲，母親Matilda De Camville Baroness Vernon35歲。他於1344年與Beatrice de Molynuex在英國英格蘭德比夏爾結婚。他們至少是5個兒子的父母。他去世於1358年在英國斯塔福爾塔沃斯享年42歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard de Vernon于大约1317年出生在英国英格兰藍卡爾洛斯托可时，他的父亲Richard de Vernon II33岁，母亲Matilda De Camville Baroness Vernon35岁。他于1344年与Beatrice de Molynuex在英国英格兰德比夏爾结婚。他们至少是5个儿子的父母。他去世于1358年在英国斯塔福爾塔沃斯享年42岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Vernon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Vernon in Eure, France, named from the Gaulish element ver(n) ‘alder’ + the Gallo-Roman locative suffix -o (genitive -ōnis).
French: habitational name from the same place as in 1 above or from any of several other places in France with the same name and etymology.

variants.name: Varnon
meanings: English (Leicestershire and Northamptonshire): variant of   Vernon  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Vernon was born about 1317, in Lostock, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Richard de Vernon II, was 33 and his mother, Matilda De Camville Baroness Vernon, was 35. He married Beatrice de Molynuex in 1344, in Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons. He died in 1358, in Tamworth, Staffordshire, England, at the age of 42.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Vernon est né environ 1317, à Lostock, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Richard de Vernon II, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Matilda De Camville Baroness Vernon, avait 35 ans. Il a épousé Beatrice de Molynuex en 1344, à Derbyshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils. Il est décédé en 1358, à Tamworth, Staffordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 42 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard de Vernon um 1317 in Lostock, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard de Vernon II 33 und seine Mutter Matilda De Camville Baroness Vernon 35. Er hat 1344 in Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom Beatrice de Molynuex geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne. Er starb 1358 im Alter von 42 Jahren in Tamworth, Staffordshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Vernon è nato il circa 1317 nel Lostock, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Richard de Vernon II, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Matilda De Camville Baroness Vernon, aveva 35 anni. Lui sposato Beatrice de Molynuex nel 1344, nel Derbyshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli. Morì nel 1358, nel Tamworth, Staffordshire, England, all'età di 42 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard de Vernonが約1317年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアロストックで生まれた時，父Richard de Vernon IIは33歳，母Matilda De Camville Baroness Vernonは35歳であった。1344年にイギリスイギリスデルブイシュアイアでBeatrice de Molynuexと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子の親であった。彼は1358年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアタムウオルスで42歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Vernon(이)가 대략 1317년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 로스톡에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard de Vernon II(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Matilda De Camville Baroness Vernon(은)는 35세였다. 그는 1344년 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르에서 Beatrice de Molynuex(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1358년 영국 스태퍼드실르 타워스에서 42세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Vernon nasceu aproximadamente 1317, em Lostock, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Richard de Vernon II, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Matilda De Camville Baroness Vernon, tinha 35 anos. Ele casou-se com Beatrice de Molynuex em 1344, em Derbyshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1358, em Tamworth, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, com 42 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Vernon родился около 1317 г. в местности лостокк, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Richard de Vernon II было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Matilda De Camville Baroness Vernon, 35. Он женился на женщине по имени Beatrice de Molynuex в 1344 г. в местности дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей. Он умер в 1358 г. в местности тамворт, стаффордшир, Англия в возрасте 42 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard de Vernon nació aproximadamente 1317, en Lostock, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Richard de Vernon II, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Matilda De Camville Baroness Vernon, tenía 35 años. Se casó con Beatrice de Molynuex en 1344, en Derbyshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos. Murió en 1358, en Tamworth, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 42 años.
